title: Bypass Trust And Workflow Entry
goal: Make closeout trust report lower trust when a broad active plan has missing package workflow evidence, even when the package is only consulted at closeout.
Clarify implement --changed as an inference-limited implementer entrypoint.
Make WORKFLOW wording more imperative and explicit about minimum orientation before implementation.
non_goals: Do not add PR templates, CI jobs, git hooks, or mandatory external enforcement.
Do not claim bypass can be detected when no later actor invokes the package.

intent.outcome: Late package consultation can identify missing workflow evidence as lower trust, and first-contact workflow text pushes agents more strongly into CLI orientation.
intent.constraints: Keep enforcement advisory and portable; do not require repo-specific review templates or CI.
intent.latitude: May adjust report payload shape, implement payload guidance, managed workflow text, tests, and docs.
intent.escalation: Stop before adding host-repo templates, CI examples as default payload, or invasive policy gates.

execution.proof: Focused CLI tests, contract checks, source/payload check, and make check passed.

invariants: Missing evidence lowers trust only when the package is consulted later.
implement --changed reports derived guidance and uncertainty, not omniscient answers.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Agents are harder to accidentally route around the package on large or complex work without making the product invasive.
intent_continuity.this slice completes the larger intended: yes for non-invasive late trust and workflow wording
intent_continuity.continuation surface: none unless implementation reveals a product gap

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Closeout trust can flag missing workflow evidence from absence, and WORKFLOW states mandatory orientation steps more clearly.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: External enforcement hooks and repo-specific templates.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: implement --changed could use the target root when selecting proof, so it now picks up config and active planning assurance instead of path-only defaults.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Implement closeout trust / bypass detection from absence; consider implement --changed; make AGENTS/WORKFLOW more imperative.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Improve workflow robustness without invasive human or CI workflow changes.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Report missing package evidence as lower trust when active broad planning exists, add implementer caveats, and harden WORKFLOW wording.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check that the package does not overclaim detection when it was never invoked.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Workspace CLI/report code, tests, managed workflow payload/current install copy, and package docs.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Focused documentation and CLI/test files only.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; make check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before adding config schema policy, default PR templates, CI generation, or hooks.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: GitHub templates, CI workflows, package install lifecycle semantics unrelated to WORKFLOW payload.

stop_conditions.stop when: Closeout trust absence detection, implementer entrypoint wording, WORKFLOW wording, and tests are landed and validated.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: Need for host-specific automation or new config schema policy emerges.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: The fix starts changing planning schema semantics broadly.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Focused tests or make check fail outside the touched surfaces.

context_budget.live working set: closeout_trust functions, implement payload, WORKFLOW payload, tests.
context_budget.recoverable later: User discussion and archived prior workflow lanes.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Add late lower-trust absence signal without external enforcement.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before committing or if tests reveal larger contract drift.

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: No PR templates, CI, hooks, or overclaiming bypass detection.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact payload field names and wording.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A config/schema policy change appears necessary.

adaptive_assurance.reason: Touches workflow guidance and closeout trust semantics, but does not mutate installed state or schemas.

requirement_refs: user feedback: closeout trust absence detection
user feedback: implement --changed as bounded entrypoint
acceptance_refs: closeout_trust reports lower trust when active planning exists but package workflow evidence is absent
implement --changed explains inference limits and next orientation route
WORKFLOW states required minimum orientation before implementation
decision_refs: PR templates and CI are deferred as invasive/repo-specific

risk_registry_refs: Do not imply the package can observe a bypass when never invoked.
invariant_refs: AGENTS.md remains a thin pointer; WORKFLOW is fallback routing, not a full manual.

durable_residue_prompts: If missing schema policy becomes necessary, route a follow-up instead of adding it opportunistically.

architecture_decision_promotion.notes: This is a behavior/writing refinement, not a new architecture decision.

active_milestone.scope: closeout trust, implement payload guidance, WORKFLOW wording

invariants: Lower-trust from absence is only visible once the package is invoked.
External enforcement remains optional and outside the default product surface.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Package workflow evidence absence lowers trust for broad active planning records.
open_questions_to_close: Which evidence absence can be detected without self-report? Active planning plus missing package workflow evidence can lower trust when closeout_trust is run.
What should implement --changed honestly infer? Paths, config, active planning assurance, and package metadata, with explicit cannot-infer limits.
How imperative should WORKFLOW be without becoming a second manual? It now requires preflight or implement --changed orientation while keeping AGENTS thin.
completion_criteria: Closeout trust emits absence-based lower-trust signal for active broad work with missing package evidence.
Implement context states inference limits and concrete orientation next step.
WORKFLOW requires at least compact orientation before implementation.
Focused tests and make check pass.

execution_run.what happened: Implemented closeout trust absence detection, added implement --changed orientation/inference-limit fields, and made WORKFLOW wording more imperative.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace CLI/report behavior, implementer context schema/reference docs, workflow payload/current install, tests, planning state
execution_run.validations run: focused pytest; contract tooling; source/payload operational install check; make check

finished_run_review.config compliance: used package workflow and checked-in planning

proof_report.validation proof: Focused CLI/conformance tests, contract tooling, source/payload boundary check, and make check.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: uv run pytest ... -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_source_payload_operational_install.py; make check

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Implement non-invasive bypass trust and stronger workflow orientation.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: closeout_trust lowers trust from missing package evidence; implement --changed exposes orientation and inference limits; WORKFLOW now states minimum orientation imperatively.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package useful as an amortized coordination layer without invasive workflow defaults.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer reportable trust signals and low-cost entrypoints over external enforcement.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can a late package invocation see enough absence to lower closeout trust honestly?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The requested non-invasive changes are implemented; invasive PR/CI/hooks remain explicitly non-goals, not unresolved slice intent.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: tests and generated implementer-context reference docs
closure_check.reopen trigger: bypass detection overclaims or workflow wording remains too optional

generated_closeout.authority: derived adapter; structured execplan fields remain authoritative

memory_learning_capture.future agents should not rediscover: Bypass detection is only late trust assessment; it requires someone to invoke closeout_trust.
memory_learning_capture.reason: Behavior is encoded in CLI output, schema docs, workflow payload, and tests.

durable_residue.learned constraint: Absence-based bypass detection cannot work unless the package is invoked later; when invoked, active planning plus missing package evidence is a lower-trust signal.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Make bypass visible without adding invasive host workflow defaults.

execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: behavior tests and generated reference docs

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: implement --changed selected proof without passing target_root, so it could miss config and active planning assurance.
signals fixed: implement --changed now passes target_root into proof selection and documents inference limits.
improvement_signal_review.next owner: none unless implementation reveals a follow-up

drift_log: 2026-05-02: Plan created before workflow robustness implementation.
